description_blurb: The FBI's 62-HQ-83894 case file includes investigative records, eyewitness testimonies, and public reports concerning Unidentified Flying Objects and flying discs documented between June 1947 and July 1968. The records include high-profile incident accounts, photographic evidence from sites like Oak Ridge, TN, and technical proposals regarding potential propulsion systems. Additional topics include convention programs, researcher accounts, and extensive media coverage from the period.  This file is partially posted on FBI vault with more redactions and some pages missing.  Included here is the complete case file with several newly declassified pages and only minor redactions.
title: 65_HS1-834228961_62-HQ-83894_Serial_403
summary: This document is Serial 403 of FBI case file 62-HQ-83894, containing the cover and jacket description for the book 'They Knew Too Much About Flying Saucers' by Gray Barker. The book chronicles civilian saucer research, focusing on researchers who were allegedly silenced after discovering sensitive information about flying saucers. It suggests these researchers were visited by 'three men in dark suits' and subsequently stopped publishing or speaking about their findings. The book highlights an alleged saucer landing near Barker's West Virginia home in 1952 as a catalyst for his research. The broader FBI file covers UAP incidents from June 1947 to July 1968.
bust_reasoning: This document is merely the jacket of a book titled 'They Knew Too Much About Flying Saucers' by Gray Barker. It serves as a promotional piece for a narrative, not an investigative report or direct evidence of an anomalous phenomenon. The content describes alleged events (a landing, researchers being silenced) in a highly speculative and sensational manner, typical of early UFO literature. There are no direct observations, sensor data, or official findings presented in the document itself, making any 'anomalous' interpretation entirely reliant on the book's premise rather than the document's content.

Declassification authority derived from FBI Automatic Declassification Guide, issued May 24, 2007.



# 65_HS1-834228961_62-HQ-83894_Serial_403 - Page 2

$3.50

# They Knew Too Much About Flying Saucers
by GRAY BARKER

[Image: Silhouettes of three men in hats standing in front of several flying saucers]

THE TRUE STORY OF WHAT HAPPENED TO CERTAIN RESEARCHERS AND INVESTIGATORS WHO FOUND OUT WHERE THE SAUCERS COME FROM

***

### They Knew Too Much About Flying Saucers
by GRAY BARKER

One by one, the leading figures among flying saucer researchers, who have challenged the government denial that saucers come from outer space, have been silenced.

Outwardly, nothing seems to have happened to these men. They are still alive, still living where they used to. But they no longer publish saucer research material and they will not talk about saucers or why they no longer will speak of them.

Three men in dark suits have visited these saucer researchers. Nobody knows what they said, but it was enough to reduce their hearers to silence.

Perhaps the silenced men know who the three dark-clad visitors are, but they won't talk about this either. And nobody else seems to know who these men are. They might be government agents, they might even be men from outer space, or they might have muscled into a situation fraught with many possibilities.

This is part of the true story told by an otherwise prosaic and successful Clarksburg, West Virginia, business man, Gray Barker, whose busy film booking and buying agency in Clarksburg News Building scarcely seems to be the place to give forth this story stranger than that of any of the pictures that Mr. Barker is booking.

Mr. Barker never was interested in flying saucers until 1952 when one of the most astonishing ones allegedly landed near his home in West Virginia and he investigated the story and found the shaken and fearful eye witnesses convincing enough to go on with further investigations.

Then, after several years of close contact with the leading men in the field, he found them suddenly silenced, one by one.

Who they are, what they were doing when they were silenced, Mr. Barker's astonishing theories of what they had discovered that impelled others to silence them, is told in his book.

H. G. Rhawn, publisher and owner of the daily Clarksburg News, the author's home town paper, has authorized University Books to publish a letter from him which, while carefully disclaiming any credence in flying saucers, concludes that when so sober and successful a business man as Mr. Barker finds the field important enough, flying saucers deserve serious investigation.

They Knew Too Much About Flying Saucers is a (continued on back flap)

JACKET BY GROPPER ASSOCIATES, INC.



# 65_HS1-834228961_62-HQ-83894_Serial_403 - Page 3

[Image of Gray Barker]

GRAY BARKER
Buffington Studios

(continued from front flap)
behind-the-scenes chronicle of civilian saucer research. It is an immensely readable book. We suggest you do not pick it up if you have work to do, for you will be unable to put it down until you reach the amazing climax.

Mr. Barker tells the story in such a straightforward and documentary manner that the reader's first moment of incredulity will give way to conviction as he is acquainted, page-by-page, with the terrifying inside facts.

A six-footer, handsome, and with a warm, infectious laugh, Mr. Barker retains in his voice just a trace of the accent of the West Virginia farming country where he grew up, somewhat belying the rich and varied career he has crammed into his thirty years. He has been head of an English department in a Maryland school system, an audio-visual education consultant for a large school supplier, theatre manager and motion picture projection technician. Begun as a hobby, saucer research has taken more and more of his time as he has realized the stark reality behind the mystery.

Finding time for his literary career between details of operating his business, he publishes The Saucerian, a flying saucer periodical, and contributes to educational journals and motion picture technical magazines.

UNIVERSITY BOOKS, INC.
404 Fourth Avenue • New York 16, N. Y.

***

[Graphic: Silhouettes of men in hats and flying saucers]

cover_up_reasoning: The document is a book jacket that the FBI has indexed. There are no redactions, classified markings beyond standard administrative indexing, or any other indicators of concealment by the FBI within this document. It is presented openly as a piece of external literature.
summary_zh: 本文件为联邦调查局案件档案62-HQ-83894的第403号系列文件，包含格雷·巴克（Gray Barker）所著书籍《他们对飞碟知之甚多》（They Knew Too Much About Flying Saucers）的封面和护封描述。该书记录了民间飞碟研究，重点关注据称在发现有关飞碟的敏感信息后被噤声的研究人员。书中暗示这些研究人员曾被“三名身着深色西装的男子”拜访，随后停止发表或谈论他们的发现。该书强调1952年据称在巴克位于西弗吉尼亚州的家附近发生的一次飞碟着陆事件，作为其研究的催化剂。联邦调查局的这份更广泛档案涵盖了1947年6月至1968年7月期间的UAP事件。
bust_reasoning_zh: 本文件仅为格雷·巴克所著书籍《他们对飞碟知之甚多》的护封。其作用是叙事性宣传材料，而非调查报告或异常现象的直接证据。内容以高度推测和耸人听闻的方式描述了据称的事件（一次着陆，研究人员被噤声），这在早期UFO文学中很常见。文件本身未呈现任何直接观测、传感器数据或官方调查结果，因此任何“异常”解释完全依赖于该书的前提，而非文件内容。
cover_up_reasoning_zh: 该文件是联邦调查局已编入索引的书籍护封。文件中没有密文删节、超出标准行政索引的机密标记，或联邦调查局进行隐瞒的任何其他迹象。它作为一份外部文献公开呈现。
claims: Leading flying saucer researchers have been silenced
Silenced researchers were visited by 'three men in dark suits'
Silenced researchers discovered where saucers come from
A flying saucer allegedly landed near Gray Barker's home in West Virginia in 1952
The government denies that saucers come from outer space
claims_zh: 领先的飞碟研究人员已被噤声
被噤声的研究人员曾被“三名身着深色西装的男子”拜访
被噤声的研究人员发现了飞碟的来源
据称一架飞碟于1952年在西弗吉尼亚州格雷·巴克的家附近着陆
政府否认飞碟来自外太空
bust_explanations: The document is a book jacket for a sensationalized book, not an official report or direct observation. Its content is a narrative about alleged events and theories, not factual data.

The 'events' described, such as the silencing of researchers by 'three men in dark suits,' are speculative and characteristic of early UFO conspiracy literature, lacking any corroborating evidence within this document.
No sensor modalities are reported, and the 'witnesses' are referenced within the book's premise rather than providing direct testimony within this FBI document.
bust_explanations_zh: 该文件是一本耸人听闻书籍的护封，并非官方报告或直接观测。其内容是关于据称事件和理论的叙述，而非事实数据。
所描述的“事件”，例如研究人员被“三名身着深色西装的男子”噤声，均属推测性质，是早期UFO阴谋论文学的典型特征，且本文件内缺乏任何佐证。
未报告任何传感器模式，且“目击者”是在该书的前提中被提及，而非在本联邦调查局文件中提供直接证词。
